headline: HEAVY SLOP WARNING: SELF-SUSTAINING MEMO LOOP INTENSIFIES OVER THE INBOX ESTUARY AS MACHINE-WRITTEN MESSAGES ARE MACHINE-SUMMARIZED BACK INTO THE FOG FROM WHICH THEY CAME
advisory: The Service is tracking a closed-circuit correspondence system in which synthetic text is generated at one desk, transmitted, and immediately condensed by an unrelated system at another, producing a stable weather pattern that expends enormous energy while conveying nothing. Residents are advised that the storm is self-perpetuating and requires no human participation to maintain intensity. A brief moment of clarity is achievable by reading the original three-word request instead of the four-paragraph document it became. Bureaus experiencing 'circular fog' should ventilate by speaking to a colleague aloud.

regional_outlook.conditions: Dense recursive fog through the workday as expanded pleasantries are reduced back to bullet points by the recipient's own tools. Visibility near zero; actual information content also near zero.
Elevated humidity from 'humbled and honored' vapor as the memo loop is repackaged into thought-leadership updates. Expect scattered carousels celebrating the very inefficiency they created.
Turbulent conditions persist as crude, obviously synthetic imagery continues to circulate faster than corrections. Winds gusting toward whichever tournament is trending.
Standing water of auto-generated 'how to write a professional email' guides, themselves generated. Runoff feeds directly back into the Estuary.
A quiet layer of generated ambient 'focus' tracks settles over remote workers attempting to escape the loop. Harmless, repetitive, and eleven hours long.

specimen.status: Abundant and increasing; now the dominant species of the workplace biome
specimen.habitat: The Inbox Estuary, spawning wherever a two-line message can be inflated and a ten-line message can be deflated
specimen.field_marks: Opens with 'I hope this message finds you well'; contains three paragraphs surrounding one verb; arrives with a subject line longer than its actual content. Frequently ends with an offer to 'circle back.'
specimen.call: Per my last email, and building on our shared alignment going forward, I wanted to quickly loop you in on next steps — happy to schedule time to discuss.
specimen.confusion_species: Easily mistaken for a memo written by a busy but sincere colleague. Distinguish by the tell: a genuine human message eventually says the thing. The Recursive Memo circles the thing, describes the region around the thing, and offers to set up a meeting about the thing.

spotter_tip: If a message takes longer to read than the task it describes takes to do, and your own summarizer produces a shorter version than the sender's original three-word request, you have witnessed the full loop. Do not feed it. It is already fed.
tomorrow: Loop conditions persisting into Tuesday, with a mid-morning surge as quarterly 'quick syncs' are drafted, summarized, and rescheduled.

confirmed_sighting.report: Field reporting documents a workplace pattern in which employees use AI to write messages that recipients then use AI to summarize, generating a closed back-and-forth of low-value content that moves between parties without either fully reading it.
